title: docker-exec floor empirical baseline — Phase A of persistent-exec-socket cycle
summary: Phase the runner-13 cycle. Phase A (this commit) builds a sweeper-safe .hexa benchmark harness (tool/runner_exec_bench.hexa) that produces an empirical p50/min/max/mean distribution per docker-exec pattern (bare / bash -c / bash -lc), with overhead-corrected timing via gdate +%s%3N pre-measured pair median. Result on hexa-exec container, 30 runs: bash_lc p50=57ms (min=33, max=103, mean=58.5). Brief carried a 103ms floor figure — that turned out to be the **max** for bash_lc, not the floor; the operational p50 is ~50-60ms. Phase B (runner-13b, deferred) will introduce persistent-mode (long-lived `docker exec -i $C bash` + line-protocol framing) to hexa_remote.sh and re-run this same harness with --persistent flag to quantify savings against this baseline.

predecessors.wave_21_2026_04_25: hexa_remote.sh docker exec isolation mode (HEXA_REMOTE_DOCKER=1) — single docker exec per call, no reuse
predecessors.omega_runner_12: runner status integration (predecessor in runner namespace)

context.problem: Brief asserted a 103ms docker-exec floor and proposed long-lived shell + line-protocol to bypass. Pre-implementation audit required: (1) verify the floor is real and reproducible, (2) characterize distribution shape (long tail? bimodal?), (3) identify which exec pattern hexa_remote actually uses.
context.scope_decision: Phase A only. Implementing a persistent-exec transport before measuring the actual savings ceiling is premature. Build the bench harness first; let the data drive Phase B sizing.

files_created.overhead_correction: gdate +%s%3N pair median pre-measured (30 samples) and subtracted from each docker sample
files_created.sentinel: __RUNNER_BENCH__ pattern=NAME runs=N p50_ms=X min_ms=Y max_ms=Z mean_ms=M overhead_corrected=true
files_created.no_side_effects: /bin/true; container untouched; nothing written to disk

bash_c.note: single outlier at 913ms inflated mean — likely host scheduling / docker daemon GC blip

bash_lc.note: this is the path hexa_remote.sh L1237 uses; the 103ms in the brief matches THIS max, not p50

interpretation: operational floor (p50) is ~50-60ms across all three patterns — surprisingly, login-shell variant is not measurably slower
long tail exists: bash_c saw a 913ms outlier in 30 runs (3% tail rate)
brief's '103ms floor' corresponds to bash_lc max (worst-case), not the typical floor
Phase B savings ceiling: ~50-60ms per call (typical) + tail-elimination (variable)

phase_b_design.target: amortize the per-call docker-exec startup by reusing one long-lived shell per session
phase_b_design.transport: bash coproc inside hexa_remote.sh (existing grandfathered .sh, raw 9 exception). Spawn once, send requests via stdin, read responses via stdout.

protocol_spec.request: newline-terminated shell command, optionally followed by sentinel close marker
protocol_spec.response_framing: command output + trailing line `__HX_DONE__ rc=<int>` to delimit
protocol_spec.lifecycle: spawn on first PERSISTENT call; reuse for subsequent calls in same hexa_remote process; teardown on EXIT trap
protocol_spec.fallback: if persistent shell exits unexpectedly, fall back to fresh docker exec (current path)
protocol_spec.error_capture: merge stderr into stdout via 2>&1 inside the coproc, separate later via marker line if needed
protocol_spec.isolation: each request runs in a subshell `(cd /tmp; <cmd>)` to keep cwd/env reset between requests; explicit opt-out via REUSE_SHELL_STATE=1

phase_b_design.integration_point_in_hexa_remote: L1237 (`REMOTE_CMD="$REMOTE_TRAP; docker exec -i $_DOCKER_CONTAINER bash -lc $(printf %q "$_INNER_CMD")"`). New env var HEXA_REMOTE_PERSISTENT=1 routes through the coproc path; default OFF preserves current behaviour exactly.
phase_b_validation: re-run tool/runner_exec_bench.hexa with --persistent flag (requires Phase B implementation)
compare p50/p95 against this baseline; target: >= 3× speedup on p50 (≤20ms) for sequential calls
concurrent-call safety: serialize via a per-session lock; persistent shell is single-client by design

validation.result: ran cleanly, all 3 patterns produced sentinel + human-readable rows

scope_constraints_honoured: hexa-only-suffix (raw 9): new file is .hexa, no .sh edits this cycle
raw 71 report-only: bench is read-only on container, no state writes
CLI-only inter-call: tool is invoked via `hexa run`, no library coupling; reads HEXA_ARGV env
no parallel-session collision: tool/runner_exec_bench.hexa is a NEW path; nexus parallel session touches state/, n6/, cli/run.hexa, tool/kick_*.hexa — all distinct
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked
minimal blast: 1 new file in nexus, no edit to hexa_remote.sh or any other tool

non_changes.scripts/bin/hexa_remote: untouched in Phase A — Phase B will add HEXA_REMOTE_PERSISTENT path here

next_followups: ω-runner-13b: implement HEXA_REMOTE_PERSISTENT mode (bash coproc + line-protocol)
ω-runner-13c: integrate persistent mode into bridge_health.sh sweep — 16 bridges × 50ms saved = 800ms per sweep
consider: hexa-runner image cold-start vs warm-start (separate axis if relevant)
